jokes.text: I want to pinch your tiny wiener. It's packer! - Oh! Pack-man, I thought you were a girl.
jokes.setup: Someone calls about wanting to pinch Michael's 'tiny wiener'
jokes.punchline: It's revealed to be Packer making a crude joke, and Michael thought he was a woman
jokes.explanation: Classic Packer crudeness with a small misdirection element. Functional but not sophisticated.

jokes.text: Everybody, we are at defcon 5.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to sound serious about the crisis
jokes.punchline: He uses DEFCON 5, which is actually the lowest alert level, opposite of what he means
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael malapropism - shows his character trying to sound authoritative while revealing ignorance. Economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: Now, we cannot let the pedophile win again.
jokes.setup: Michael's clip is second most watched after a teacher wrongfully accused of being a pedophile
jokes.punchline: Michael frames this as a competition he must win against 'the pedophile'
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark Michael logic - turning a serious news story into personal competition. Shows his narcissism and tone-deafness perfectly.

jokes.text: Get it up. That's what-- Let's do it.
jokes.setup: Michael telling everyone to get the website up
jokes.punchline: He starts to make a 'that's what she said' joke but stops himself
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about Michael's own catchphrase. Shows rare self-awareness while still being Michael.

jokes.text: Really? A baby otter? Okay, um, count me in as 'who cares?'
jokes.setup: Everyone is distracted by cute baby otter video instead of watching Michael's clip
jokes.punchline: Michael's bitter dismissal of something universally adorable
jokes.explanation: Michael's petty jealousy over a baby animal getting more attention. Good character consistency and relatability.

jokes.text: It's not even that interesting, a baby otter. It can't even stand up. It's trying to stand up. There it goes.
jokes.setup: Michael dismissing the baby otter video
jokes.punchline: He gets progressively more invested while trying to remain dismissive
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing Michael's inability to maintain his position. The progression from dismissive to engaged is beautifully written.

jokes.text: Cheap, foreign printers attacking innocent Americans.
jokes.setup: Jo describing the printer fire story
jokes.punchline: She personifies printers as foreign attackers in an almost xenophobic way
jokes.explanation: Establishes Jo's folksy but business-savvy character through absurd framing of the crisis.

jokes.text: I'm not sure you do, Teddy bear. Well, now I think I might not.
jokes.setup: Michael claims to know what happened
jokes.punchline: Jo's dismissive pet name makes Michael immediately doubt himself
jokes.explanation: Shows the power dynamic perfectly - Jo's casual condescension immediately deflates Michael's confidence.

jokes.text: Put your hand up, Norma Rae.
jokes.setup: Looking for the whistleblower
jokes.punchline: Jo references the famous union organizing movie when calling out the leaker
jokes.explanation: Smart cultural reference that shows Jo's age and business background while being economical.

jokes.text: If you say anything, so help me God, I'll break off the temples of your glasses and stick 'em in your eye sockets.
jokes.setup: Dwight threatening someone not to confess
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and violent threat involving eyeglasses
jokes.explanation: Peak Dwight - creatively violent and oddly specific. The glasses detail makes it memorably absurd.

jokes.text: Even if I thought that our printers killed baby seals, I would not be a whistle-blower.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining his loyalty to the company
jokes.punchline: He'd stay loyal even if the company committed an adorable animal genocide
jokes.explanation: Shows Dwight's extreme loyalty through an absurd hypothetical. Good escalation to something universally sympathetic.

jokes.text: The Bernards, for generations, have silenced whistle-blowers. That's how we made all our money. Woody Guthrie wrote a song about us.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining his family's anti-whistleblower tradition
jokes.punchline: Claims the famous folk protest singer wrote about his family's corruption
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist touch - Woody Guthrie famously wrote protest songs, so having him write about the Schrutes is perfect irony.

jokes.text: ♪ Old Mr. Bernard ♪ ♪ Who have you silenced today? ♪
jokes.setup: Dwight claiming Woody Guthrie wrote about his family
jokes.punchline: He actually sings a verse of this fictional protest song
jokes.explanation: Exceptional commitment to the bit. Rainn Wilson's delivery would be perfect, and the lyrics perfectly capture Guthrie's style while being about Dwight's family.

jokes.text: I know when their birthdays are. I know what their favorite kind of cake is. I know what color streamers they like. All that's just birthday information, Michael.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to prove he knows his employees well
jokes.punchline: Jo points out all his examples are just birthday party planning
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Michael's false confidence. Jo's matter-of-fact response is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Well, nine times out of ten, that's the anus they checked.
jokes.setup: Jo explaining how her mother identified guilty prisoners
jokes.punchline: Crude revelation about prison body cavity searches as interrogation technique
jokes.explanation: Crude but character-appropriate for Jo's rough background. Gets a shock laugh but isn't sophisticated.

jokes.text: We should give 'em a one-way ticket to Montego Bay... Where they keep all the Al-Qaeda. Uh, that's, uh, Guantanamo Bay.
jokes.setup: Michael suggesting what to do with the whistleblower
jokes.punchline: He confuses Montego Bay (vacation spot) with Guantanamo Bay (prison)
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael malapropism. The correction makes it funnier - accidentally suggesting they send someone on vacation instead of prison.

jokes.text: Wha--unh! Dwight, what the hell? - Apache persuasion hold- - that's the hell.
jokes.setup: Nick trying to use Dwight's computer
jokes.punchline: Dwight puts him in some kind of martial arts hold with an absurd name
jokes.explanation: Pure Dwight - unnecessary violence with a made-up martial arts name. Physical comedy that fits the character perfectly.

jokes.text: And like I'm gonna believe one of his 'spermed' lovers.
jokes.setup: Angela dismissing claims about Andy
jokes.punchline: She uses 'spermed' as a crude adjective for Andy's romantic partners
jokes.explanation: Angela's repressed sexuality comes out in awkward clinical language. Crude but character-appropriate cringe.

jokes.text: are you referring to alchemy?
jokes.setup: Jo asking if Dwight is turning money into more money
jokes.punchline: Dwight thinks she's talking about the medieval pseudo-science of turning base metals into gold
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight - taking a common business expression literally and historically. Shows his medieval mindset.

jokes.text: I swear on the graves of my parents, who aren't even dead yet. That's a little much.
jokes.setup: Andy trying to prove his innocence with a dramatic oath
jokes.punchline: He realizes mid-swear that his parents are still alive, making it ridiculous
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke where Andy gets carried away and realizes his mistake mid-speech. Self-aware without breaking character.

jokes.text: Yeah, I did. I-I was talking to this girl at a bar. Oh, no, no, no... I think she could sense my sadness.
jokes.setup: Darryl confessing to being the leak
jokes.punchline: He pathetically explains he was trying to impress a woman by talking about printer fires
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Darryl's loneliness and poor pickup strategies. Character-driven but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Was she cute? - No. Oh, God, Darryl!
jokes.setup: Michael asking about the woman Darryl told
jokes.punchline: Darryl's 'no' makes Michael even more exasperated
jokes.explanation: Michael's priorities perfectly revealed - the crime is worse because she wasn't even attractive. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: And if that is not poetic justice, I don't know what is.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining that Darryl, his best employee, will get fired
jokes.punchline: He calls this 'poetic justice' which is the opposite of what it actually is
jokes.explanation: Michael completely misusing 'poetic justice' shows his tendency to use phrases he doesn't understand.

jokes.text: Why would a man who hates people want to have a relationship with a maid? Oh, uh, I don't know. They way I look at it, there's only one of two reasons. He knows a secret about her that she doesn't know herself, or he wants to use her services to mop up after a murder.
jokes.setup: Jo discussing Dwight's mystery novel
jokes.punchline: She provides two sinister explanations for the romance plot
jokes.explanation: Jo's dark interpretation of romance shows her cynical business mind. Good character moment.

jokes.text: Write your own damn novel.
jokes.setup: Jo giving detailed analysis of Dwight's novel plot
jokes.punchline: Dwight's defensive response suggests Jo basically solved his entire story
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight deflection when caught. The economy of the line suggests Jo nailed his plot.

jokes.text: Her husband's a reporter. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Jim explaining why he told someone about the printers
jokes.punchline: The casual revelation that of course the woman's husband is a reporter
jokes.explanation: The matter-of-fact delivery of this terrible coincidence is funny but not exceptional.

jokes.text: My mind is going a mile an hour. - That fast?
jokes.setup: Jim saying his mind is racing with worry
jokes.punchline: Pam points out that a mile an hour is actually very slow
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline with great economy. Pam's deadpan correction of the cliché is expertly timed.

jokes.text: I weirdly know exactly what you're saying to me. So I'll see you in Meredith's van in five minutes.
jokes.setup: Jim confessing to Michael about being a whistleblower
jokes.punchline: Michael's response suggests this scenario has happened before and they have a protocol
jokes.explanation: Implies this exact situation has happened before. The specific mention of Meredith's van adds perfect absurd detail.

jokes.text: I have never seen so many parking tickets.
jokes.setup: Characters meeting in Meredith's van
jokes.punchline: Visual gag of Meredith's van being covered in parking tickets
jokes.explanation: Visual gag that reinforces Meredith's chaotic lifestyle. Solid but not exceptional.

jokes.text: This situation all up in hee-yah is what's messed up.
jokes.setup: Darryl responding to Jim saying the situation is messed up
jokes.punchline: Darryl's attempt at street slang with 'hee-yah' sounds awkward
jokes.explanation: Mild character moment showing Darryl trying too hard to sound cool. Not particularly strong.

jokes.text: Guys, I couldn't help it. It is so boring where we work. I mean, it's as interesting as a morgue. It might be less interesting than a morgue.
jokes.setup: Ryan explaining why he leaked information
jokes.punchline: He escalates the boredom comparison from morgue to potentially less interesting than a morgue
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Ryan's pretentious attitude about the job. The morgue comparison is morbidly funny.

jokes.text: I phone, I woof. 'woof' is a site that I'm launching to be the last word in social networking.
jokes.setup: Ryan listing his communication methods
jokes.punchline: He mentions 'woof' as if it's a real platform
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ryan - always working on some doomed startup. 'Woof' as a social network name is appropriately stupid.

jokes.text: Ryan, you have a woof on line one. Thank you, Erin. Woof!
jokes.setup: Ryan demonstrating his 'woof' platform
jokes.punchline: Erin plays along with the demonstration, and Ryan responds with enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Erin's earnest participation in Ryan's nonsense is sweet. The 'woof' callback works well.

jokes.text: That's just what we need, another black man in prison.
jokes.setup: Discussion of turning in Darryl
jokes.punchline: Michael's well-intentioned but awkward racial comment
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael racial comment - well-intentioned but tone-deaf. Creates appropriate cringe.

jokes.text: Basement office? You mean like a lair?
jokes.setup: Dwight describing property features
jokes.punchline: Jo's matter-of-fact interpretation of a basement office as a supervillain lair
jokes.explanation: Jo's deadpan delivery makes this funnier. She immediately sees the sinister potential Dwight would love.

jokes.text: Do we give 'em a Christmas bonus? I say... Yes. It's Christmas.
jokes.setup: Michael describing punishment for the whistleblower
jokes.punchline: Even while planning punishment, his kindness makes him want to give a Christmas bonus
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael moment - his innate kindness overrides his attempt to be tough. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Speak to me. Speak.
jokes.setup: Jo frustrated with Michael's silence
jokes.punchline: She talks to him like he's a dog who won't obey
jokes.explanation: Jo's authoritative nature comes through in treating Michael like a pet. Mildly amusing.

jokes.text: Are you gonna kill me?
jokes.setup: Jo taking Michael somewhere private
jokes.punchline: Michael's paranoid fear that Jo might murder him
jokes.explanation: Michael's paranoia about Jo is character-appropriate. Gets a nervous laugh.

jokes.text: Make 'em an offer they can't refuse. No, on second thought, lowball 'em.
jokes.setup: Dwight giving real estate instructions using Godfather language
jokes.punchline: He immediately undercuts the dramatic movie reference with practical business advice
jokes.explanation: Great Dwight moment - starts with movie drama then pivots to practical cheapness. Perfect character contradiction.

jokes.text: I have an early dinner that I need to get to... With the chief of police.
jokes.setup: Michael making excuses about where he needs to go
jokes.punchline: He adds an important-sounding detail to make himself seem more important
jokes.explanation: Michael's need to sound important through fake connections. Character-appropriate but not exceptional.

jokes.text: What a rich timbre your voice has.
jokes.setup: Dwight interviewing Stanley
jokes.punchline: He makes an oddly formal compliment about Stanley's voice quality
jokes.explanation: Dwight's formal, awkward attempt at interrogation. The word 'timbre' is perfectly pretentious for him.

jokes.text: Can we all agree to say that it was Andy for now and sit with it, see how it feels?
jokes.setup: Dwight trying to conclude his investigation
jokes.punchline: He suggests they blame Andy experimentally, like trying on clothes
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - treating a false accusation like a lifestyle choice to test. The phrasing is perfectly awkward.

jokes.text: I bought a video camera last year, and I was looking at the tapes, and there were only, like, 12 minutes that I felt was worth taping the whole year. And most of that was just birds in my condo complex.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining his rough year to Jo
jokes.punchline: His year was so uneventful that only 12 minutes were worth filming, mostly birds
jokes.explanation: Painfully relatable and sad. Michael's mundane loneliness comes through perfectly in this specific detail.

jokes.text: It's not been a blockbuster year for me financially. My blockbuster stock is down.
jokes.setup: Michael describing his financial troubles
jokes.punchline: He makes an unintentional pun about Blockbuster stock being down
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay that Michael doesn't realize he's making. Shows his poor investment choices historically.

jokes.text: But I'm not here to talk about that. I am here to talk about 'suck it.'
jokes.setup: Gabe about to confess to being whistleblower
jokes.punchline: Abrupt topic change to promote his invention
jokes.explanation: Gabe's inability to read the room and constant promotion of his invention

jokes.text: I think we all can agree that it's either Gabe or Angela. It's Angela. Get her, boys.
jokes.setup: Dwight suggesting suspects for the leak
jokes.punchline: Kevin immediately picks Angela and calls for mob justice
jokes.explanation: Kevin's immediate mob mentality is character-appropriate. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: Phyllis, what's this guy's name again? I don't know. Is it 'shadow' or 'Garth'? It's something weird. I-- My name is Nick!
jokes.setup: Michael asking for the IT guy's name
jokes.punchline: They guess completely wrong names while he's standing right there
jokes.explanation: Classic office cringe - not knowing a coworker's name. The wrong guesses make it worse.

jokes.text: We liked the last guy, sadiq, because he kept to himself. And we also thought he might have been a terrorist.
jokes.setup: Michael explaining why they liked the previous IT guy
jokes.punchline: He casually mentions they suspected him of terrorism
jokes.explanation: Michael's casual racism/xenophobia delivered matter-of-factly. Creates appropriate cringe but relies on offensive stereotypes.

jokes.text: Inner-city kids use computers for two things-- games and porn.
jokes.setup: Nick explaining his teaching plan
jokes.punchline: His cynical assessment of what kids actually use computers for
jokes.explanation: Nick's bitter parting shot. Cynical but not particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: And you definitely can't fit into a size two.
jokes.setup: Nick delivering parting insults
jokes.punchline: He makes a cruel personal attack on someone's weight/size
jokes.explanation: Mean-spirited parting shot that's more cruel than funny. Gets uncomfortable laughs but isn't well-crafted.

jokes.text: And they'd make a Barbie out of me.
jokes.setup: Jo explaining her business aspirations
jokes.punchline: She wanted to be successful enough that Mattel would make a Barbie doll of her
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly sweet and specific aspiration. Shows Jo's human side through an absurd but relatable goal.

jokes.text: Nobody will want to play with my Barbie.
jokes.setup: Jo explaining why she doesn't want to be known for the recall
jokes.punchline: She imagines children rejecting her hypothetical Barbie doll
jokes.explanation: Sweet continuation of the Barbie concept. Humanizes Jo through her concern about her legacy with children.

jokes.text: I-I surely do. And don't call me honey.
jokes.setup: Michael offering to do the press conference
jokes.punchline: He quotes Airplane while trying to sound serious
jokes.explanation: Michael can't help but quote movies even in serious moments. Shows his inability to be genuinely serious.

jokes.text: I own a 1/8 share on a rental property down in Pittston. Well, I'm 1/8 proud of you.
jokes.setup: Dwight bragging about his partial property ownership
jokes.punchline: Jo's proportional pride response
jokes.explanation: Brilliant mathematical wordplay. Jo's quick wit deflates Dwight's boasting perfectly with proportional mockery.

jokes.text: Enjoy that chair for now... Because pretty soon you will be on your feet, like Buckingham Palace.
jokes.setup: Dwight threatening Jo about the property purchase
jokes.punchline: His metaphor makes no sense - Buckingham Palace is a building, not someone standing
jokes.explanation: Peak Dwight absurdity - trying to sound threatening with a completely nonsensical metaphor. The confidence makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Does that make me a hero? No, it does not!
jokes.setup: Andy explaining he didn't want children to die
jokes.punchline: He denies being a hero for the bare minimum of human decency
jokes.explanation: Andy's false modesty about basic human decency. Good character moment showing his need for recognition.

jokes.text: It just seemed like the right thing to do. Yeah.
jokes.setup: Pam praising Andy for going to the press
jokes.punchline: Andy accepts praise for something he didn't actually do
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - Andy gets credit he doesn't deserve while the real whistleblowers remain hidden. Better on rewatch.
